yes, that's the point. armor being a stat that has diminishing returns means they're going to eventually reach a point where it's just trivial to build armor or DR any further. and if they fine tune the values to a point that feels decent, then we won't be dealing with beefcakes anymore. i just threw out those values to explain diminishing returns.

referenced_message.author: la_ni
referenced_message.content:
idea: cap damage reduction to 40% in PvP modes (including sources from perks/talents/OS etc...) and convert extra DR beyond that point to flat armor with diminishing returns. 

ex: 
40-50% DR = 50 armor per 1% dmg reduction over cap
50%-60% DR = 25 armor per 1% dmg reduction over cap
60-50% DR = 10 armor per 1% dmg reduction over cap
etc...

since armor itself has diminishing returns , this makes stacking dmg reduction less effective while also maintaining the efficacy (somewhat) of the ones who have already built for maximizing dmg reduction. the exact numbers should of course be tuned so that people aren't walking beef cakes still.

fixing dmg reduction won't fix pvp. damage is out of control right now. it makes playing any class in pvp without sustain a pain in the ass because there's no counterplay. if you do happen to get an edge people can just run for ages until they heal back up or run emergency measures and they're back to full health...

content:

referenced_message.author: chewybarka
referenced_message.content:
I, for one, think a 50% DR hard cap is too high.
The Bulwark already has access to self-healing, a shield that blocks incoming damage, crowd control through stuns, and enough survivability to avoid taking meaningful damage for extended periods. At that point, they're not just a tank—they're effectively a one-man party.
Lowering the DR hard cap to 30% would make Bulwarks take more damage, but when you consider the rest of their toolkit, they would still be a strong and viable class. They just wouldn't be so strong that they can stand against 12 players in the DZ by themselves.
No class in the game should be capable of winning a 1v12. A coordinated group with a good team composition might be able to win a 4v12, but no single player should be able to create a build that allows them to consistently take on 12 players alone.

content:
I, for one, think a 50% DR hard cap is too high.
The Bulwark already has access to self-healing, a shield that blocks incoming damage, crowd control through stuns, and enough survivability to avoid taking meaningful damage for extended periods. At that point, they're not just a tank—they're effectively a one-man party.
Lowering the DR hard cap to 30% would make Bulwarks take more damage, but when you consider the rest of their toolkit, they would still be a strong and viable class. They just wouldn't be so strong that they can stand against 12 players in the DZ by themselves.
No class in the game should be capable of winning a 1v12. A coordinated group with a good team composition might be able to win a 4v12, but no single player should be able to create a build that allows them to consistently take on 12 players alone.

referenced_message.author: chewybarka
referenced_message.content:
This is the only division game without soft and gard caps. Also the only division game where bugs win over skill and hard grind time. Introducing caps will prevent that fully. 
The only stat that says going over is beneficial is mutishot. Right now the ways to get to 100% mutishot are broken. The Dominating Presences OS doesn't work. So the argument of it stops the stats that are supposed to function from over stack is mute. The main focus here is not allowing tanks to fully negate damage while they use pummeling sheild to pera-stun a target to death. 
With stat caps the triangle is restored. Dps can kill healers, healers can out last tanks, tanks can kill dps. 
Without that triangle in place, it allows players to abuse mechanics and ruin experiences for everyone.

Without that triangle in place, it allows players to abuse mechanics and ruin experiences for everyone.

referenced_message.content: i disagree with this, there are stats that already mention in their description that going over 100% allows for a second proc and not to mention that while yes vang builds wouldnt have their dmg effected one of the reasons why they are so hard to counter is also due to them building dr to live longer, they will become more glass cannons  from a dr cap and therefore easier to kill aswell, by capping every stat it would require a re-rebalance after everything is capped this would be due to some stats needing a 120% cap and others only a 50% cap etc and that just doesnt work too well... while yes some other attributes in my opinion should also have caps i do not find it viable for everything to have a cap as it would severely dimmish everyone's access to actually finish mission, try running your main build but caps on your stats and see how much harder pve is - pve mobs dont scale off your attribute stats at all and therefore they are the main viable way for vangs to kill them well, for medics to heal enough that people dont get 1 shot, for jugs to live long enough to pull agro, for tach pharma to actually deal dmg and etc

referenced_message.author: chewybarka
referenced_message.content:
Yes, I believe Defense Reduction needs adjustment, but I do not believe it should be changed in isolation.
The larger issue is that players can stack certain attributes to extremely high values. If Defense Reduction is reduced without addressing the rest of the stat system, it risks creating new balance problems. For example, tank-focused builds could become significantly weaker while high-damage builds remain dominant.
Instead, I believe the game would benefit from soft caps and hard caps across all major attributes.
For example:
Weapon Critical Chance: 30% soft cap, 50% hard cap.
Skill Critical Chance: 45% soft cap, 60% hard cap.
Skill builds could reasonably have higher caps because skills generally do not deal damage as frequently as weapons.
The same philosophy could be applied to other attributes, including:
Weapon Critical Damage
Weapon Multishot Chance
Skill Critical Damage
Skill Multishot Chance
Defense Reduction
Damage Reduction
Other high-impact combat statistics
Once players reach a soft cap, additional investment would provide reduced returns. Hard caps would prevent any single attribute from reaching unhealthy levels.
The benefit of this approach is that it addresses the overall balance of the stat system rather than targeting a single attribute. Damage-focused builds, tank builds, and skill builds would all see reductions in extreme stat stacking, creating a healthier balance across the game as a whole.
In my opinion, the goal should not be to weaken one specific build type, but to prevent any build from reaching a point where a single stat becomes overwhelmingly powerful. A system of soft caps and hard caps would accomplish that while still allowing players meaningful freedom in how they build their characters.

A system of soft caps and hard caps would accomplish that while still allowing players meaningful freedom in how they build their characters.

referenced_message.author: jbcode
referenced_message.content:
Yes, DR needs to be capped. This game should not allow for builds where a player be indefinitely immortal regardless of how much damage is applied to them.

Things like using Blitz Tactics + Unflinching + Emergency Measures makes Jug & Medic super tanky, but still they can be downed with enough damage applied which is good. However, if a player can stack over 100% DR, they just are immortal and that should never be the case.

Every other complaint people bring up about other classes should be addressed and balanced separately but they are not a 100% immunity, they can all be countered by existing game mechanics so are not as drastic an issue as damage reduction stacking to me.
